notes:
CRITICAL — SCOPE CLEAN: Diff touches exactly one file: src/renderer/game/scenes/WorldMapScene.js. The _renderLandmarkLayer call site and method body visible in the working tree are correctly attributed to world-landmarks (parallel batch); path-renderer's diff hunk is isolated to the import line (+hash2), the _renderPathLayer call site (~lines 156-169 of diff), removal of the old per-loop lineBetween block (~lines 162-167 pre-patch), and the new _renderPathLayer method body (lines 286-419 in tree). No terrain rendering, node placement, click handlers, HUD, or scene transitions were touched.

CRITICAL — JS ONLY: No .ts/.tsx files added. No package.json modification.

CRITICAL — PURE REPLACEMENT: Old straight-line block (centerX/centerY/line/lineBetween) deleted from the locations loop. No parallel toggle remains. Confirmed in diff: 4-line old block removed, single _renderPathLayer call inserted.

CRITICAL — PATH DRAWN BEFORE NODES: _renderPathLayer call at line 160 of tree, which is before the `for (const loc of locations)` node loop at line 182. Confirmed.

CRITICAL — WATER SKIPPED: `if (biomeA === BIOME.WATER || biomeB === BIOME.WATER) continue;` at line 342 of tree. Correct. TODO(ferry) comment present.

CRITICAL — DETERMINISTIC CURVE DIRECTION: hash2(0x9a7b, seedA, seedB) at line 393. seedA/seedB are sum-of-charCodes of loc.id and 'neighborhood' respectively — stable across reloads. hash2 confirmed exported from utils/terrainNoise.js (line 19). Sign: odd hash → +1, even → -1.

HIGH — BEZIER CURVE: Quadratic bezier approximated with 20 lineTo segments. Control point is midpoint + perpendicular offset at 12% of segment length. Correct formula at lines 410-415 (u²A + 2ut·ctrl + t²B).

HIGH — COLOR BY BIOME: Switch on majorityBiome at lines 351-371. DESERT 0xa08250/0.6, GRASSLAND 0x6e5a32/0.65, MOUNTAIN 0x3c3228/0.75, default 0x786446/0.6. Palette matches spec's rgba values exactly (converted to hex integers). Locked paths multiply alpha by 0.35.

HIGH — PATH WIDTH: DESERT=2, GRASSLAND=2, MOUNTAIN=3, default=2. Spec asked for 2-3 desert/grassland and 3-4 mountain; worker chose lower bounds. Acceptable — lower bound is within range. Recovery audit flagged this as PARTIAL; reviewer concurs it is within spec tolerance, not a revision trigger.

HIGH — LAYER ORDER: _pathContainer.setDepth(-50) at line 319. Sits above terrain container (depth -100 per terrain layer) and below nodes (no explicit depth = 0 / creation order). Call sequence in create(): _renderTerrainLayer → _renderPathLayer → _renderLandmarkLayer → node loop. Correct.

MEDIUM — DASHED PENDING PATHS: Not implemented. TODO comment at lines 306-308 documents dependency on data/discoveryUnlocks.js which does not yet exist. This is explicitly allowed by the spec ('Skip if discoveryUnlocks isn't present yet'). Follow-up needed when world-discovery-quests authors that file.

MEDIUM — PERFORMANCE: No performance.now() bracket in _renderPathLayer. Spec did NOT set a hard ms target, and the method is ~60 lineTo calls (trivial). Recovery estimate of <2ms is credible. Not a blocking issue; world-map-polish can add a bracket if profiling is desired.

LOW — ARCHITECTURAL FIT: _renderPathLayer follows the same method signature pattern as _renderTerrainLayer and _renderLandmarkLayer. Single batched Graphics object. Container pattern matches terrain layer. No imports from cameras/ or game3d/.

LOW — MINOR DEAD-CODE RISK: homeX/homeY declarations were lifted above the _renderPathLayer call. The old duplicate declarations below (for neighborhood home-base marker) were replaced with a comment. Confirmed no dangling duplicate declarations remain.

RECOVERY RECEIPT ACCURACY: All synthesized fields match observed code. files_changed=['src/renderer/game/scenes/WorldMapScene.js'] correct. Symbol line numbers in recovery receipt (line 160 call site, line 310 method, line 393 hash) match actual file within ±3 lines — acceptable for a synthesized receipt. The diff stat (351 insertions/10 deletions) matches git diff output. Sibling confirmation from world-landmarks is plausible given the parallel-batch context. Recovery methodology was sound.

FOLLOW-UP NOTES FOR world-map-polish: (1) Add performance.now() bracket around _renderPathLayer for telemetry. (2) Review curve aesthetics when non-desert regions are added — current map has only arizona_desert+mountain_range so paths are visually uniform. (3) Consider strokeWidth=3 for DESERT/GRASSLAND if paths read too thin at high DPI.

FOLLOW-UP NOTES FOR world-discovery-quests: When data/discoveryUnlocks.js is authored, trigger a follow-up pass on WorldMapScene._renderPathLayer to implement dashed strokes for loc.pending === true paths per spec item 5.
